architecture.summary: NF-CoT embeds an autoregressive normalizing flow inside an LLM backbone to model continuous chain-of-thought reasoning. During training, explicit CoT traces are encoded by a frozen VAE into continuous codes, then reparameterized through shallow invertible flow blocks into an LLM-facing thought space. This thought space is modeled by a causal Gaussian density parameterized by an NF head, while answer tokens are generated by the standard LM head, both sharing the same LLM backbone in a unified causal stream. At inference, continuous thoughts are sampled autoregressively left-to-right from the flow's density, then the model switches to the LM head to decode answers using the cached KV states, all without iterative denoising or second backbone pass.

claims.dataset: MBPP, MBPP+, HumanEval, HumanEval+, LiveCodeBench v6
claims.statement: NF-CoT (Unified) improves pass@1 on code generation benchmarks, achieving 68.8% average across MBPP, MBPP+, HumanEval, HumanEval+, and LiveCodeBench v6 compared to base model 55.8%

claims.dataset: MBPP, MBPP+, HumanEval, HumanEval+, LiveCodeBench v6
claims.statement: NF-CoT (Unified) outperforms LaDiR (diffusion-based latent reasoning baseline) by 7.1% on average pass@1 across the same five code benchmarks
NF-CoT inference is 1.92× faster than LaDiR on HumanEval (325.6 seconds vs 625.3 seconds total for 16 candidates per problem)
NF-CoT training throughput is 2.85× higher than LaDiR (18.4 samples/s vs 6.45 samples/s during Stage 2)
NF-CoT per-sample FLOPs are 19.9 TFLOPs on HumanEval inference compared to LaDiR's 49.3 TFLOPs, representing 2.48× reduction

claims.dataset: MBPP, MBPP+, HumanEval, HumanEval+, LiveCodeBench v6
claims.statement: NF-CoT + RL (GRPO refinement) achieves 70.1% average pass@1 across five benchmarks, improving from 68.8% without RL
NF-CoT achieves 59.0 pass@128 on MBPP+ compared to base model's 53.0, and LaDiR's 58.0, indicating better pass@k scaling

Validation focuses on code-generation benchmarks; extending to other reasoning tasks remains future work. NF-CoT is trained on fixed-length VAE-encoded trajectories from explicit-CoT traces, so the learned density inherits their biases, and a fixed latent budget may not fit all problems.
